title: hackelia macrantha
intro: Hackelia macrantha, also known as Largeflower Stickseed, is a perennial flowering plant in the borage family that is native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Hackelia macrantha include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Hackelia macrantha include teas and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water.
